title: NetworkClient's stateful resync/retry self-heal orchestration is never instantiated or exercised as a unit
evidence: `client/src/client/NetworkClient.ts` (1100 LOC, churn 29 - highest in repo per inventory.md) implements `setFiring`/`recoverStuckFire`/`resyncLog` (lines 554-629) and `submitAction`'s seq-conflict retry/backoff loop (lines 720-808) - the fail-open desync self-heal path the code comments themselves flag as subtle ("if resync applies our shot and self-heals... Only if the shot is STILL unresolved after the resync... do we release the lock", line ~549-553). `scripts/checks/lockstep.mjs` explicitly states 'This does NOT instantiate NetworkClient (it is coupled to Supabase + rAF); instead it reproduces the EXACT sequencing discipline NetworkClient now uses' - i.e. a parallel re-implementation is tested, not the class itself. Grep across scripts/checks/*.mjs and client/src for `new NetworkClient(` finds zero matches outside the class file's own constructor. Only isolated pure sub-pieces are covered: `shouldBufferSeq` (resync_guard.mjs), `postOnceWithRetry` (netretry.mjs), `replayNetworkAction` (netrounds.mjs, batteries.mjs). The integration of these pieces inside `resyncLog`/`recoverStuckFire`/`submitAction` - including the AbortController timeout path (line 600-616), the stale-nextExpectedSeq race guard at line 625, and the MAX_SEQ_RETRIES/SEQ_BACKOFF_MS loop - has no test exercising the assembled class.
impact: A regression in the self-heal race handling (e.g. the fire lock never releasing after a failed resync, or a retry storm past MAX_SEQ_RETRIES) would manifest as stuck turns or desync in live networked play, the exact failure class this code was written to prevent (per its own comments referencing prior bugs #57). Only sub-pieces are unit tested, so a wiring bug between them is invisible to `npm run check`.
recommendation: Extract the resync/retry orchestration behind an injectable transport (Supabase client + fetch already flow through `this.supabase`), and add a harness that constructs a NetworkClient with a stub/mock supabase client to drive: resyncLog abort/timeout, resyncLog re-fetch success flushing pendingActions, submitAction seq_conflict retry up to MAX_SEQ_RETRIES then failFire, and setFiring/recoverStuckFire's self-heal-vs-genuine-failure branch.
acceptance_criteria: A test/harness instantiates NetworkClient (or an equivalent extracted orchestration unit) with a mocked transport and exercises the resync-then-flush success path and the exhausted-retries failure path
The self-heal race (shot resolved by resync vs. genuinely lost) has an assertion distinguishing the two outcomes
